response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with nearly two decades of experience, but his profile is fundamentally a web/full-stack one (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) and misaligned with this hands-on C++ role. His only professional C++ work was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list. He has no evident MQTT, message-broker, industrial-protocol, or IEC 62443/SDL experience, which are core to this industrial-automation position. His low-latency systems work in DLang and his mentoring record are genuine assets, but they do not offset the missing core competencies.
strengths: Extensive and varied engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong mentoring, testing, and craftsmanship culture (TDD, workshops, conference talks) plus React/Node.js familiarity
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months ending in 2015); C++ absent from current skills list
No MQTT, message-broker, or industrial-protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No Secure Development Lifecycle or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity exposure; cloud background is AWS rather than Azure

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation, MQTT, and IEC 62443 security experience
